summary: Iranian-backed militias launched a fiber-optic FPV drone attack on the US Victory Base near Baghdad International Airport, Iraq. Ukrainian forces are deploying advanced drone platforms like the Banderyk-24 and SHADOX, designed for modular combat roles including reconnaissance and precision strikes, while also testing humanoid Phantom MK-1 robots from Foundation in battlefield conditions. Russian forces are countering with systems like the Elka anti-drone platform, which has been effective against Ukrainian fixed-wing Hornets, and are adapting by using log-periodic antennas on Molniyas to resist jamming and improve range. Ukrainian drone tactics are disrupting Russian logistics and rear infrastructure, forcing both sides to innovate in electronic warfare and drone countermeasures, with fiber-optic drones proving especially resilient to jamming.
